factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across several segments, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Volume (in thousands) 1,592 1,551 41 3% ... Revenue increased $251 million to $2.9 billion from the prior year's first quarter due to volume growth, pricing gains and increased fuel surcharge.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable, driven by volume increases and pricing gains.

evidence.text: Revenue increased $251 million to $2,869 million from $2,618 million in the prior year's first quarter, reflecting a 10% increase.

factors.summary: The company successfully raised prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue increased $251 million to $2.9 billion from the prior year's first quarter due to volume growth, pricing gains and increased fuel surcharge.

factors.summary: Restructuring charges and efficiency savings are delivering measurable savings, with a $173 million restructuring charge and efficiency savings of $74 million.

evidence.text: The Company reduced the management workforce and completed other restructuring activities that resulted in a restructuring charge of $173 million. ... Efficiency savings of $74 million were driven by lower T&E and operating support costs.

factors.summary: Operating margins are stable, with a slight increase in the operating ratio indicating some margin compression.

evidence.text: Operating Income $712 $704 $8 1% ... Operating Ratio 75.2% 73.1% (210) bps

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in chemicals and intermodal, driven by volume growth and pricing gains.

evidence.text: Revenue increased $251 million to $2.9 billion from the prior year's first quarter due to volume growth, pricing gains and increased fuel surcharge. ... Intermodal 688 678 1% ... Revenue Per Unit (Dollars) 2017 2016 % Change 2017 2016 % Change

factors.summary: Agricultural and food products, chemicals, automotive, and intermodal segments are growing, while forest products are declining.
Fuel costs increased significantly due to price hikes and volume growth, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Fuel expense increased $68 million due to the following: •A 45 percent price increase drove $67 million in additional fuel expense. •Volume-related costs were $6 million higher due to a five percent year-over-year increase in gross ton miles.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with slight decreases in some categories due to restructuring and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: Labor and Fringe expense decreased $7 million due to the following: •Efficiency savings of $74 million were driven by lower T&E and operating support costs.

factors.summary: Talent retention is stable as the company focuses on training and technology investments to maintain employee satisfaction.

evidence.text: The Company measures and reports safety and service performance. The Company strives for continuous improvement in these measures through training, innovation and investment.

factors.summary: Management has not provided specific forward guidance but is focused on executing Precision Scheduled Railroading to drive future performance.

evidence.text: CSX is adjusting its strategy to successfully execute this new model, relentlessly focusing on providing customer service, controlling costs, operating safely, developing people and optimizing assets.

factors.summary: Management is confident in their new strategy, as evidenced by their focus on Precision Scheduled Railroading and cost-saving initiatives.

evidence.text: CSX is adjusting its strategy to successfully execute this new model, relentlessly focusing on providing customer service, controlling costs, operating safely, developing people and optimizing assets.

factors.summary: The long-term strategic direction is focused on Precision Scheduled Railroading, with significant investments in technology and infrastructure to support this model.

evidence.text: On March 6, 2017, the Company named E. Hunter Harrison as its new CEO and began implementing Precision Scheduled Railroading. As a result, CSX is adjusting its strategy to successfully execute this new model...

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, economic conditions affecting coal and intermodal demand, and competitive pressures in various segments.

evidence.text: Fuel expense increased $68 million due to the following: •A 45 percent price increase drove $67 million in additional fuel expense. ... Domestic Utility Coal - Volume declined due to mild winter weather and a competitive loss of short-haul interchange traffic...

factors.summary: Implementation of Precision Scheduled Railroading is a key catalyst expected to drive future efficiency and cost savings.

evidence.text: The Company named E. Hunter Harrison as its new CEO and began implementing Precision Scheduled Railroading. As a result, CSX is adjusting its strategy to successfully execute this new model...

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with operational improvements in safety and service performance, though there are challenges in certain segments like coal and forest products.

evidence.text: CSX’s FRA reportable personal injury frequency index of 0.99 for the quarter was nine percent unfavorable, despite a slight reduction in the number of injuries, due to a significant decline in man-hours from fewer employees...

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is improving through efficiency initiatives and asset optimization, as indicated by lower dwell times and increased train velocity.

evidence.text: Train Velocity 20.2 21.1 (4)% Dwell 26.1 26.0 —%

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable with no significant build-up or drawdown, supporting efficient operations.

evidence.text: Working capital can also be considered a measure of a company's ability to meet its short-term needs. CSX had a working capital surplus of $ 383 million and $ 447 million as of March 31, 2017 and December 30, 2016, respectively.

factors.summary: Quality and safety performance has shown improvement in certain metrics, such as train accident rates, but challenges remain in others like on-time arrivals.

evidence.text: FRA Train Accident Rate 2.37 3.14 25% ... On-Time Arrivals 61% 64% (5)%

factors.summary: Market share is stable or improving in several segments, particularly intermodal and chemicals, due to volume growth and competitive gains.
Interest rate impact is neutral as the company's average interest rates have decreased, partially offsetting higher debt balances.

evidence.text: Interest expense decreased $6 million primarily due to lower average interest rates, partially offset by higher average debt balances.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving due to higher collections and efficient capital management, supporting future investments and shareholder returns.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities increased $289 million primarily driven by higher collections of freight accounts receivable and the timing of tax, payroll and interest payments.
evidence.subsection: Material Changes in Consolidated Balance Sheets and Significant Cash Flows

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to increase with a focus on infrastructure and technology, particularly for Positive Train Control, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Projected capital investments for 2017 are now expected to be $2.1 billion, including approximately $270 million for Positive Train Control.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation risks across multiple areas, including fuel surcharges, environmental issues, taxes, personal injuries, and occupational illnesses. These risks could have a material impact on its financial condition and results of operations.

evidence.text: •the outcome of litigation, claims and other contingent liabilities, including, but not limited to, those related to fuel surcharge, environmental matters, taxes, shipper and rate claims subject to adjudication, personal injuries and occupational illnesses;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents CSX CORPORATION ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: The company faces mixed economic conditions affecting its operations. While there are positive developments in the industrial and automotive sectors, challenges persist in the coal sector due to mild weather and competition.

evidence.text: The company is facing potential economic headwinds due to changes in domestic or international economic conditions, which could impact the transportation industry.

evidence.text: Domestic Coke, Iron Ore and Other - Iron ore shipments were down as a large customer retooled its production, but overall demand was stable.
Automotive - Volume increased, driven by SUV and truck shipments, as North American vehicle production increased versus the prior year at several CSX-served plants.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes pose significant risks to CSX Corporation, particularly in areas such as transportation, environment, taxation, and compliance with laws like PTC implementation. These changes could lead to increased costs, penalties, and operational challenges, impacting the company's financial condition and results of operations. The risks are further exacerbated by potential noncompliance and the unpredictability of legislative and regulatory developments, which could affect the company's ability to maintain operational and financial stability.

evidence.text: •legislative, regulatory or legal developments involving transportation, including rail or intermodal transportation, the environment, hazardous materials, taxation and initiatives to further regulate the rail industry;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents CSX CORPORATION ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: •the cost of compliance with laws and regulations that differ from expectations (including those associated with PTC implementation), as well as costs, penalties and operational and liquidity impacts associated with noncompliance with applicable laws or regulations;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents CSX CORPORATION ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: •natural events such as severe weather conditions, including floods, fire, hurricanes and earthquakes, a pandemic crisis affecting the health of the Company's employees, its shippers or the consumers of goods, or other unforeseen disruptions of the Company's operations, systems, property or equipment;

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks related to labor relations, particularly concerning labor stoppages that could disrupt operations and affect customer deliveries. The evidence highlights labor and benefit costs as well as potential difficulties arising from labor issues, which may lead to operational challenges.

evidence.text: •labor and benefit costs and labor difficulties, including stoppages affecting either the Company's operations or customers' ability to deliver goods to the Company for shipment;

factors.summary: The company identifies a range of near-term risks, including legislative and regulatory developments, economic conditions, competition, compliance costs, natural events, and labor costs. Additionally, there are specific risks related to competitive pressures in certain segments and challenges in converting truck traffic to rail due to pricing and service issues.

evidence.text: •legislative, regulatory or legal developments involving transportation, including rail or intermodal transportation, the environment, hazardous materials, taxation and initiatives to further regulate the rail industry; •the outcome of litigation, claims and other contingent liabilities, including, but not limited to, those related to fuel surcharge, environmental matters, taxes, shipper and rate claims subject to adjudication, personal injuries and occupational illnesses; •changes in domestic or international economic, political or business conditions, including those affecting the transportation industry (such as the impact of industry competition, conditions, performance and consolidation) and the level of demand for products carried by CSXT; •natural events such as severe weather conditions, including floods, fire, hurricanes and earthquakes, a pandemic crisis affecting the health of the Company's employees, its shippers or the consumers of goods, or other unforeseen disruptions of the Company's operations, systems, property or equipment; •competition from other modes of freight transportation, such as trucking and competition and consolidation or financial distress within the transportation industry generally; •the cost of compliance with laws and regulations that differ from expectations (including those associated with PTC implementation), as well as costs, penalties and operational and liquidity impacts associated with noncompliance with applicable laws or regulations; •the impact of increased passenger activities in capacity-constrained areas, including potential effects of high speed rail initiatives, or regulatory changes affecting when CSXT can transport freight or service routes; •unanticipated conditions in the financial markets that may affect timely access to capital markets and the cost of capital, as well as management's decisions regarding share repurchases; •changes in fuel prices, surcharges for fuel and the availability of fuel; •the impact of natural gas prices on coal-fired electricity generation; •availability of insurance coverage at commercially reasonable rates or insufficient insurance coverage to cover claims or damages; •the inherent business risks associated with safety and security, including the transportation of hazardous materials or a cybersecurity attack which would threaten the availability and vulnerability of information technology; •adverse economic or operational effects from actual or threatened war or terrorist activities and any governmental response; •labor and benefit costs and labor difficulties, including stoppages affecting either the Company's operations or customers' ability to deliver goods to the Company for shipment; •the Company's success in implementing its strategic, financial and operational initiatives; •changes in operating conditions and costs or commodity concentrations; and •the inherent uncertainty associated with projecting economic and business conditions.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents CSX CORPORATION ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

evidence.text: Truck competition from excess capacity continued to constrain growth despite positive momentum in the housing market and rail shipments of building products. ... Crude by rail economics remained challenged.
